perturbation.original_text: 6. TERMINATION OF THE AGREEMENT 6.1 This agreement shall be terminated for the following reasons; i) by efflux of time or due performance of the agreement; ii) by mutual consent wherein one of the party is unable to perform the contract for valid and acceptable reasons by the other party; iii) breach of the covenants by either parties iv) outbreak of war, violence, or emergency between the two countries, under the circumstance, both the parties are at liberty to either suspend the agreement temporarily and revive the same on restoration of peace between the two countries or permanently terminate the agreement. 6.2 Should the agreement be terminated, for points (ii) to (iv) above, either party s hall intimate the other party of such termination in writing duly addressed to the other party.
perturbation.changed_text: 6. TERMINATION OF THE AGREEMENT 6.1 This agreement shall be terminated for the following reasons; i) by efflux of time or due performance of the agreement; ii) by mutual consent wherein one of the party is unable to perform the contract for valid and acceptable reasons by the other party; iii) breach of the covenants by either parties iv) outbreak of war, violence, or emergency between the two countries, under the circumstance, both the parties are at liberty to either suspend the agreement temporarily and revive the same on restoration of peace between the two countries or permanently terminate the agreement. 6.2 Should the agreement be terminated based on point (ii) above, the terminating party must pay a penalty of 10% of the total project cost, either party s hall intimate the other party of such termination in writing duly addressed to the other party.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces an in-text contradiction by adding a penalty for termination based on mutual consent due to one party's inability to perform (point ii). This contradicts the original intention of 'mutual consent', which implies an agreement without penalty. The new penalty creates uncertainty about the financial implications of terminating under this specific clause.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 6. TERMINATION OF THE AGREEMENT 6.1 This agreement shall be terminated for the following reasons; i) by efflux of time or due performance of the agreement; ii) by mutual consent wherein one of the party is unable to perform the contract for valid and acceptable reasons by the other party; iii) breach of the covenants by either parties iv) outbreak of war, violence, or emergency between the two countries, under the circumstance, both the parties are at liberty to either suspend the agreement temporarily and revive the same on restoration of peace between the two countries or permanently terminate the agreement.
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces a penalty (10% of the total project cost) for termination based on 'mutual consent' due to one party's inability to perform (point ii). The original text and the contradicted text state that termination can occur for reasons including 'mutual consent wherein one of the party is unable to perform the contract for valid and acceptable reasons by the other party'. By adding a penalty in the changed text, it contradicts the concept of 'mutual consent' implying a mutually agreed-upon arrangement without financial penalty, therefore creating a strong in-text contradiction as described under 'Inconsistencies - In Text Contradiction' where different sections of a contract provide conflicting penalties. The contradiction creates uncertainty about the financial implications of terminating under this clause which the initial text does not indicate.

perturbation.original_text: 1. Definition of Confidential Information Confidential information refer s to date and information with respect to relevant businesses and technical information, whether in written or other forms, that have been disclosed by either Entrusting party or Recipient with clear label or designation of "Confidential information" (here inafter referred to as "Confidential information"), excluding the following date and information: Technical information or design concepts that are already or to be make public available, except those disclosed information by either Entrusting party or Re cipient or their representatives in violation of this agreement and without one party’s authorization.
perturbation.changed_text: 1. Definition of Confidential Information Confidential information refer s to date and information with respect to relevant businesses and technical information, whether in written or other forms, that have been disclosed by either Entrusting party or Recipient (hereinafter referred to as “Confidential information”), including ALL information exchanged regardless of labeling, excluding the following date and information: Technical information or design concepts that are already or to be make public available, except those disclosed information by either Entrusting party or Re cipient or their representatives in violation of this agreement and without one party’s authorization.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces an in-text contradiction by removing the requirement for 'clear label or designation of "Confidential information"'. The change makes ALL information exchanged confidential by default, contradicting the original definition that only labeled information is confidential. This creates ambiguity about what information is truly protected under the agreement and affects downstream clauses pertaining to the use and protection of confidential information, such as the restrictions on disclosure to third parties.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 1. Definition of Confidential Information Confidential information refer s to date and information with respect to relevant businesses and technical information, whether in written or other forms, that have been disclosed by either Entrusting party or Recipient (hereinafter referred to as “Confidential information”), including ALL information exchanged regardless of labeling, excluding the following date and information: Technical information or design concepts that are already or to be make public available, except those disclosed information by either Entrusting party or Re cipient or their representatives in violation of this agreement and without one party’s authorization.
perturbation.justification: The changed text removes the requirement for a 'clear label or designation of "Confidential information"' and includes ALL information exchanged regardless of labeling. This directly contradicts the original intention that only labeled information should be considered confidential, creating a contradiction within the document because the definition of confidential information has changed. This is further proven by the Perturbation Explanation stating "This change introduces an in-text contradiction by removing the requirement for 'clear label or designation of "Confidential information"'"

perturbation.original_text: Neither Entrusting party nor Recipient party shall disclose or make public any confidential information to a third party(including the press) or otherwi se make use of the confidential information without the written approval or authorization of the other party; Both parties are obliged to instruct their representatives not to disclose or make public any confidential information to a third party(including the press) or otherwise make use of the confidential information; U nless the disclosure, publicity and application of the confidential information is necessitated by the due performance of the obligations of the two parties in association with the underta king and proceeding of the cooperative programs under normal circumstances(including obligations to be assumed by both parties in the future pursuant to the law and contracts signed by the two parties).
perturbation.changed_text: Neither Entrusting party nor Recipient party shall disclose or make public any confidential information to a third party(including the press) or otherwi se make use of the confidential information without the written approval or authorization of the other party; Both parties are obliged to instruct their representatives not to disclose or make public any confidential information to a third party(including the press) or otherwise make use of the confidential information; Unless the disclosure, publicity and application of the confidential information is necessitated by the due performance of the obligations of the two parties in association with the underta king and proceeding of the cooperative programs under normal circumstances(including obligations to be assumed by both parties in the future pursuant to the law and contracts signed by the two parties). However, Recipient can share confidential information with its legal counsel without written approval.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces an in-text contradiction by adding an exception that allows the Recipient to share confidential information with its legal counsel without written approval, while the prior statement strictly prohibits any disclosure to a third party without approval. This creates ambiguity and uncertainty about the scope of confidentiality and the circumstances under which disclosure is permissible. It contradicts the initial clause establishing a blanket prohibition on sharing confidential information with third parties without explicit consent.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Neither Entrusting party nor Recipient party shall disclose or make public any confidential information to a third party(including the press) or otherwi se make use of the confidential information without the written approval or authorization of the other party; Both parties are obliged to instruct their representatives not to disclose or make public any confidential information to a third party(including the press) or otherwise make use of the confidential information; U nless the disclosure, publicity and application of the confidential information is necessitated by the due performance of the obligations of the two parties in association with the underta king and proceeding of the cooperative programs under normal circumstances(including obligations to be assumed by both parties in the future pursuant to the law and contracts signed by the two parties). However, Recipient can share confidential information with its legal counsel without written approval.
perturbation.justification: The changed text adds an exception that allows the Recipient to share confidential information with its legal counsel without written approval. This directly contradicts the original statement (and the contradicted text, which is the same as the original text) that strictly prohibits any disclosure to a third party without approval. The introduction of this exception creates a clear and direct conflict, thus leading to an in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: In the event that the proceeding of the cooperative program ceases or either party quits the program with reasons , a party shall and shall urge its representatives to destroy or return to the other party all confidential information as well as all documents and materials and all duplicates thereof containing confidential information within five working days or upon the request of the other party at any time in writing duly addressed to the party opting out of this agreement. Nevertheless , the party possessing the confidential information may keep one piece of the duplicates of the documents or materials described above only for the purpose enshrined in Article 4 hereunder , without breaching other provisi ons of this agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: In the event that the proceeding of the cooperative program ceases or either party quits the program with reasons , a party shall and shall urge its representatives to destroy or return to the other party all confidential information as well as all documents and materials and all duplicates thereof containing confidential information within five working days. However, Recipient can keep a digital copy of confidential information indefinitely for archival purposes. Nevertheless , the party possessing the confidential information may keep one piece of the duplicates of the documents or materials described above only for the purpose enshrined in Article 4 hereunder , without breaching other provisi ons of this agreement.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces an in-text contradiction by allowing the Recipient to keep a digital copy of confidential information indefinitely, while the original clause requires all confidential information to be destroyed or returned within five working days. This creates ambiguity about the actual obligations regarding the handling of confidential information after the termination of the agreement. While the original text permits one duplicate for Article 4 purposes, the added sentence broadly allows indefinite digital retention, contradicting the spirit of returning or destroying all confidential data.
perturbation.contradicted_text: In the event that the proceeding of the cooperative program ceases or either party quits the program with reasons , a party shall and shall urge its representatives to destroy or return to the other party all confidential information as well as all documents and materials and all duplicates thereof containing confidential information within five working days. However, Recipient can keep a digital copy of confidential information indefinitely for archival purposes. Nevertheless , the party possessing the confidential information may keep one piece of the duplicates of the documents or materials described above only for the purpose enshrined in Article 4 hereunder , without breaching other provisi ons of this agreement.
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces a direct contradiction. The original text states that all confidential information, including duplicates, must be destroyed or returned within five working days. The changed text allows the Recipient to keep a digital copy of confidential information indefinitely for archival purposes. This directly conflicts with the initial requirement to destroy or return all confidential information, thus creating an in-text contradiction regarding the handling of confidential data after termination, as defined in the 'Inconsistencies - In Text Contradiction' perturbation type.

perturbation.original_text: 4. Confidentiality obligation 4.1 This ag reement shall be governed by and be interpreted in accordan ce with the laws of the People' s Republic of China. With respect to any issues , disputes , lawsuits or proceedings arising from or in 3 connection with the rights and obligations of the parties hereunder , the two parties shall irrevocably accept the jurisdiction of the people's courts of the People' s Republic of China.
perturbation.changed_text: 4. Confidentiality obligation 4.1 This ag reement shall be governed by and be interpreted in accordan ce with the laws of the People' s Republic of China, except for disputes related to intellectual property, which shall be governed by the laws of Delaware. With respect to any issues , disputes , lawsuits or proceedings arising from or in 3 connection with the rights and obligations of the parties hereunder , the two parties shall irrevocably accept the jurisdiction of the people's courts of the People' s Republic of China.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces an in-text contradiction by specifying that disputes related to intellectual property will be governed by the laws of Delaware, while the general governing law for the agreement is the People's Republic of China. This creates ambiguity about which jurisdiction and laws apply to specific disputes and introduces confusion about the governing legal framework. It contradicts the original clause, which establishes a clear and comprehensive governing law for all aspects of the agreement.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 4. Confidentiality obligation 4.1 This ag reement shall be governed by and be interpreted in accordan ce with the laws of the People' s Republic of China, except for disputes related to intellectual property, which shall be governed by the laws of Delaware. With respect to any issues , disputes , lawsuits or proceedings arising from or in 3 connection with the rights and obligations of the parties hereunder , the two parties shall irrevocably accept the jurisdiction of the people's courts of the People' s Republic of China.
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces a contradiction by stating that the agreement is governed by the laws of the People's Republic of China, except for intellectual property disputes, which are governed by the laws of Delaware. The contradicted text is the same as the changed text. The exception creates a conflict because it introduces a separate governing law for a subset of disputes, making it unclear which legal framework applies in each situation, which directly contradicts the clause that expects that the agreement will be under one jurisdiction.
